evals.prompt: Create a local-only dev.sh for a repo with postgres, redis, a Python API, a worker, and a Vite frontend. I want one command to boot everything in tmux, with clean reruns and logs, but I do not want any remote proxy setup.
evals.expected_output: A local-first bash script that launches the stack in tmux, avoids remote-proxy assumptions, captures pane IDs, writes per-run logs, and adds dependency checks for the API/worker.
assertions: The script does not make Tailscale or another remote proxy mandatory for the default path
The script captures tmux pane IDs instead of using numeric pane indices
The script creates timestamped logs and refreshes logs/latest
The script includes readiness checks for dependent services

evals.prompt: Rewrite my fragile tmux launcher. It currently targets panes by index, stacks duplicate pipe-pane commands on rerun, and drops keystrokes when I rerun it quickly. Keep the same services but make it idempotent and operator-friendly.
evals.expected_output: A rewritten tmux launcher that kills and recreates windows safely, uses pipe-pane -o, adds a short post-split delay, and keeps reruns stable.
assertions: The rewritten script tears down the target window before recreating it
The script uses tmux pipe-pane -o for pane log piping
The script includes a short delay before tmux send-keys
The script avoids numeric pane targeting

evals.prompt: Build a tmux-based dev bootstrap for a monorepo with postgres, redis, API, worker, web app, and Storybook. Group the panes so they stay readable. I also need optional Tailscale Serve support behind an explicit flag because I only use it sometimes.
evals.expected_output: A multi-window bash launcher that separates services by concern, keeps no more than four panes per window, and gates Tailscale Serve behind an explicit opt-in flag.
assertions: The script uses multiple named tmux windows for readability
No tmux window exceeds four panes
Tailscale Serve is opt-in rather than always-on
The script prints local URLs and, when enabled, a remote URL
